title: hieracium pilosum subsp. favrei
intro: Hieracium pilosum subsp. favrei, also known as pilosella, is a plant species within the dandelion-like genus of Hieracium, characterized by its hairy stems and yellow flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may be attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Hieracium pilosum subsp. favrei are said to include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some cases, it is also used in tinctures and salves to leverage its medicinal properties.
